description: Evaluates privileged access management service maturity—PAM systems, KPIs/KRIs, application and device privileged access, service custodianship, and SOC logging of privileged account activity.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A privileged access management service exists and is reviewed frequently for efficacy.

capabilities.name: PAM system
capabilities.description: A PAM system manages privileged accounts enterprise-wide with lifecycle automation, configuration review, and integration with applications, endpoints, and network devices.
evaluation_focus: PAM system deployed for privileged accounts
Automated PAM user lifecycle and access
Integration with applications, endpoints, and network devices

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: PAM KPIs and KRIs are documented and reviewed for efficacy.

capabilities.name: Privileged Account Access
capabilities.description: Privileged access to application resources is formalized, unified across assets, automated via self-service portals, and reviewed frequently.

capabilities.name: Device Privilege access
capabilities.description: Privileged escalation to endpoint resources is formalized, unified across assets, automated via self-service portals, and reviewed frequently.

capabilities.name: Privilege account service custodian
capabilities.description: A dedicated PAM service manager owns privileged accounts rather than the IAM engineer or custodian.
evaluation_focus: Separation from IAM engineer or custodian ownership

capabilities.name: PAM logging
capabilities.description: Privileged account activity and lifecycle changes are logged securely to the SOC, including dynamic changes.
evaluation_focus: Documented PAM activity logging to SOC
Secure privileged account logging to SOC
Dynamic privileged account changes to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
